016package org.consensusj.jsonrpc.introspection;
017
018import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.node.NullNode;
019import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcError;
020import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcErrorException;
021import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcException;
022import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcRequest;
023import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcResponse;
024import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcService;
025import org.jspecify.annotations.Nullable;
026import org.slf4j.Logger;
027import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
028
029import java.lang.reflect.Method;
030import java.lang.reflect.Modifier;
031import java.util.List;
032import java.util.Collections;
033import java.util.ArrayList;
034import java.util.Map;
035import java.util.Arrays;
036import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;
037import java.util.stream.Collectors;
038
039import static org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcError.Error.INVALID_PARAMS;
040import static org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcError.Error.METHOD_NOT_FOUND;
041import static org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcError.Error.SERVER_EXCEPTION;
042
043/**
044 * Interface and default methods for wrapping a Java class with JSON-RPC support. The wrapper is responsible for
045 * extracting the JSON-RPC {@code method} name and {@code params}from the request, calling the appropriate method
046 * in the wrapped object and wrapping the {@code result} in a {@link JsonRpcResponse}.
047 * <p>
048 * The wrapped class must contain one or more asynchronous methods that return {@link CompletableFuture}s for objects that represent
049 * JSON-RPC {@code result} values. They are mapped to JSON objects when serialized (via <b>Jackson</b> in the current implementation.)
050 * <p>
051 * This interface contains a {@code default} implementation of {@link JsonRpcService#call(JsonRpcRequest)} that
052 * uses {@link JsonRpcServiceWrapper#callMethod(String, List)} to call the wrapped service object.
053 * <p>
054 * Implementations must implement:
055 * <ul>
056 *     <li>{@link #getServiceObject()} to return the wrapped object (singleton)</li>
057 *     <li>{@link #getMethod(String)} to return a {@link Method} object for the named JSON-RPC {@code method}.</li>
058 * </ul>
059 * <p>
060 * The trick to <b>GraalVM</b>-compatibility is to use the {@code static} {@link JsonRpcServiceWrapper#reflect(Class)} method in your
061 * implementation at (static) initialization time so the reflection is done at GraalVM compile-time.
062 */
063public interface JsonRpcServiceWrapper extends JsonRpcService {
064    Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(JsonRpcServiceWrapper.class);
065
066    /**
067     * Get the service object.
068     * <p>Implementations will return their configured service object here.
069     *
070     * @return the service object
071     */
072    Object getServiceObject();
073
074    /**
075     * Get a {@link Method} object for a named JSON-RPC method
076     * @param methodName the name of the method to call
077     * @return method handle (or {@code null} if not found)
078     */
079    @Nullable
080    Method getMethod(String methodName);
081
082    /**
083     * Handle a request by calling method, getting a result, and embedding it in a response.
084     * 
085     * @param req The Request POJO
086     * @return A future JSON RPC Response
087     */
088    @Override
089    default <RSLT> CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<RSLT>> call(final JsonRpcRequest req) {
090        log.debug("JsonRpcServiceWrapper.call: {}", req.getMethod());
091        CompletableFuture<RSLT> futureResult = callMethod(req.getMethod(), req.getParams());
092        return futureResult.handle((RSLT r, Throwable ex) -> resultCompletionHandler(req, r, ex));
093    }
094    
095    /**
096     * Map a request plus a result or exception into a response with result or exception.
097     * Uses {@link #exceptionToError(Throwable)} to map exceptions to {@link JsonRpcError}.
098     * @param req The request being serviced
099     * @param result A result object or null
100     * @param ex exception or null (should not be a JsonRpcException)
101     * @param <RSLT> type of result
102     * @return A JsonRpcResponse with result or error appropriate
103     */
104    private <RSLT> JsonRpcResponse<RSLT> resultCompletionHandler(JsonRpcRequest req, @Nullable RSLT result, @Nullable Throwable ex) {
105        if (ex != null) {
106            return wrapError(req, exceptionToError(ex));
107        } else if (result != null) {
108            return wrapResult(req, result);
109        } else {
110            // Success case with a Java `void`/`Void` result which is a JavaScript `null` result.
111            //noinspection unchecked
112            return (JsonRpcResponse<RSLT>) wrapNullResult(req);
113        }
114    }
115
116    /**
117     * Map an exception (typically from callMethod) to an Error POJO
118     *
119     * @param ex Exception returned from "unwrapped" method
120     * @return An error POJO for insertion in a JsonRpcResponse
121     */
122    private JsonRpcError exceptionToError(Throwable ex) {
123        if (ex instanceof IllegalArgumentException) {
124            // This assumes the IllegalArgumentException was from mh.invoke
125            return JsonRpcError.of(INVALID_PARAMS, ex);
126        } else if (ex instanceof NoSuchMethodError) {
127            // This assumes the NoSuchMethodError was from callMethod
128            return JsonRpcError.of(METHOD_NOT_FOUND, ex);
129        } else {
130           return JsonRpcError.of(SERVER_EXCEPTION, ex);
131        }
132    }
133
134    /**
135     * Call an "unwrapped" method from an existing service object
136     * 
137     * @param methodName Method to call
138     * @param params List of JSON-RPC parameters
139     * @return A future result POJO
140     */
141    private <RSLT> CompletableFuture<RSLT> callMethod(String methodName, List<@Nullable Object> params) {
142        log.debug("JsonRpcServiceWrapper.callMethod: {}", methodName);
143        CompletableFuture<RSLT> future;
144        final Method mh = getMethod(methodName);
145        if (mh != null) {
146            List<@Nullable Object> completeParams = addNullParams(mh.getParameterCount(), params);
147            try {
148                @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
149                CompletableFuture<RSLT> liveFuture = (CompletableFuture<RSLT>) mh.invoke(getServiceObject(), completeParams.toArray());
150                future = liveFuture;
151            } catch (Throwable throwable) {
152                log.error("Exception invoking service object: ", throwable);
153                future = CompletableFuture.failedFuture(throwable);
154            }
155        } else {
156            log.warn("No such method: {}", methodName);
157            future = CompletableFuture.failedFuture(new NoSuchMethodError(methodName));
158        }
159        return future;
160    }
161
162    /**
163     * Fills in any omitted optional params from a given param list with nulls
164     *
165     * @param numParams Total number of params a method expects
166     * @param paramsFromNet Param list which may omit unused optional params
167     * @return Param list which does not omit unused optional params
168     */
169    private List<@Nullable Object> addNullParams(int numParams, List<@Nullable Object> paramsFromNet) {
170        int nullParamsNeeded = Math.max(0, numParams - paramsFromNet.size());
171        if (nullParamsNeeded > 0) {
172            List<@Nullable Object> combined = new ArrayList<>(paramsFromNet);
173            combined.addAll(Collections.nCopies(nullParamsNeeded, null));
174            return Collections.unmodifiableList(combined);
175        } else {
176            return paramsFromNet;
177        }
178    }
179
180
181    // TODO: Create a mechanism to return a map with only the desired remotely-accessible methods in it.
182    // For server-side JSON-RPC we should migrate away from CompletableFuture to Virtual Threads
183    // We should have (or generate) a mapping from lower-case JSON-RPC method names to Java camel-case names,
184    // so this `reflect` method can use a List (or Map) data structure and see if Method::name is present
185    // in the list.
186    /**
187     * Use reflection/introspection to generate a map of methods.
188     * Generally this is called to initialize a {@link Map} stored in a static field, so the reflection can be done
189     * during GraalVM compile-time.
190     * @param apiClass The service class to reflect/introspect
191     * @return a map of method names to method objects
192     */
193    static Map<String, Method> reflect(Class<?>  apiClass) {
194        return Arrays.stream(apiClass.getMethods())
195                .filter(m -> Modifier.isPublic(m.getModifiers()))               // Only public methods
196                .filter(m -> m.getReturnType().equals(CompletableFuture.class)) // Only methods that return CompletableFuture
197                .collect(Collectors
198                        .toUnmodifiableMap(Method::getName, // key is method name
199                                (method) -> method,         // value is Method object
200                                (existingKey, key) -> key)  // if duplicate, replace existing
201                );
202    }
203
204    /**
205     * Wrap a Result POJO in a JsonRpcResponse
206     * @param req The request we are responding to
207     * @param result the result to wrap
208     * @return A JsonRpcResponse with a result
209     */
210    private static <RSLT> JsonRpcResponse<RSLT> wrapResult(JsonRpcRequest req, RSLT result) {
211        return new JsonRpcResponse<>(req, result);
212    }
213
214    /**
215     * Wrap a {@code null} result in a JsonRpcResponse. Note that in JSON-RPC there is a distinction
216     * between the {@code result} field not being present and the {@code result} field
217     * returning a JavaScript {@code null}. We represent a JavaScript {@code null} with the Jackson
218     * {@code NullNode} type.
219     * @param req The request we are responding to
220     * @return A JsonRpcResponse with a null result (using Jackson {@link NullNode}
221     */
222    private static JsonRpcResponse<NullNode> wrapNullResult(JsonRpcRequest req) {
223        return new JsonRpcResponse<>(req, NullNode.getInstance());
224    }
225
226    /**
227     * Wrap a JsonRpcError in a JsonRpcResponse
228     * @param req The request we are responding to
229     * @param error the error to wrap
230     * @return A JsonRpcResponse with an error
231     */
232    private static <RSLT> JsonRpcResponse<RSLT> wrapError(JsonRpcRequest req, JsonRpcError error) {
233        return new JsonRpcResponse<>(req, error);
234    }
235}
